title: Protect Veteran Jobs Act
plain_summary: The Protect Veteran Jobs Act helps veterans who lost their federal government jobs unfairly since January 20, 2025. If a veteran was fired without a good reason, this bill lets them get their job back. The bill also makes government agencies tell Congress every 90 days how many veterans they fired and why they fired them. This helps protect veterans who work for the government and makes sure they are treated fairly.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protect Veteran Jobs Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Reinstatement Eligibility for Veteran Federal Employees; Executive Agency Reports on Removal of Veterans
sections.text: Any person who served in the military and worked for the federal government can get their job back if they were fired without a good reason between January twentieth two thousand twenty five and when this law is passed. They can get back their old job or a different federal job they are qualified for. Starting sixty days after this law is passed, and then every ninety days until January twentieth two thousand twenty nine, the head of each government agency must send a report to Congress. The report must show how many veterans who worked for that agency were fired or let go during that time period. The report must also explain why each veteran was fired or let go. In this section, the appropriate congressional committees means the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs and the Committee on Veterans Affairs in the Senate, and the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform and the Committee on Veterans Affairs in the House of Representatives. Civil service means federal government jobs as defined in section twenty one oh one of title five of the United States Code. Executive agency means government agencies as defined in section one oh five of title five of the United States Code. Veteran means a person who served in the military as defined in section one oh one of title thirty eight of the United States Code.
